title: Go Packages
content:
### Go Packages

A package is a collection of Go source files in the same directory.

### Package Declaration

Every Go file starts with a package declaration:

```go
package mathutil
```

### Naming Conventions

- Package names are lowercase, single words
- No underscores or mixed case
- Name matches the import path's last element

### Package Structure

```
myproject/
  go.mod          -- module: example.com/myproject
  main.go         -- package main
  calc/
    add.go        -- package calc
    subtract.go   -- package calc
  utils/
    strings.go    -- package utils
```

### Calling Functions from Other Packages

```go
// calculator/add.go
package calculator

func Add(a, b int) int {
    return a + b
}

// main.go
package main

import "example.com/myproject/calculator"

func main() {
    sum := calculator.Add(2, 3)
}
```

### Exported vs Unexported

| Naming | Visibility |
|--------|------------|
| Capitalized | Exported (public) |
| Lowercase | Unexported (private to package) |

```go
package mypackage

var ExportedVar = 42     // accessible outside package
var unexportedVar = 10   // only accessible within package

func ExportedFunc() {}   // exported
func unexportedFunc() {} // unexported
```

### Package main

The `main` package is special - it defines an executable program:

```go
package main

func main() {
    // entry point
}
```

### Internal Packages

The `internal` package is special - it restricts who can import it:

```
myproject/
  internal/
    auth/
      auth.go  -- only importable by siblings of myproject/
  web/
    handler.go  -- can import myproject/internal/auth
  cmd/
    main.go     -- can also import
```

### Blank Import

Import a package solely for its `init()` side effects:

```go
import _ "github.com/lib/pq"
```

> **Key Point:** All files in the same directory must belong to the same package. A package can be split across multiple files. Only exported names are accessible outside the package.

quiz.question: What makes a function exported from a package?
Can files in the same directory have different package names?
options: No, must be the same package
